4.3.3.name: Key PostgreSQL Functions for Table Decomposition and Normalization

4.3.4.name: Key PostgreSQL Functions for Table Decomposition and Normalization

4.4.2.answer:
-- Create Employees Table
CREATE TABLE Employees (
    emp_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    emp_name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL
);

-- Create Projects Table
CREATE TABLE Projects (
    project_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    project_name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL
);

-- Create EmployeeProjectAssignments Table
CREATE TABLE EmployeeProjectAssignments (
    emp_id INT NOT NULL,
    project_id INT NOT NULL,
    hours_worked INT NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (emp_id, project_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (emp_id) REFERENCES Employees(emp_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES Projects(project_id)
);

-- Insert data into Employees table
INSERT INTO Employees (emp_id, emp_name) VALUES
(1, 'John Doe'),
(2, 'Jane Smith'),
(3, 'Bob White');

-- Insert Data into Projects Table
INSERT INTO Projects (project_id, project_name) VALUES
(101, 'Website Redesign'),
(102, 'Mobile App'),
(103, 'Data Migration');

-- Insert Data into EmployeeProjectAssignments Table
INSERT INTO EmployeeProjectAssignments (emp_id, project_id, hours_worked) VALUES
(1, 101, 20),
(1, 102, 15),
(2, 101, 25),
(2, 103, 10),
(3, 102, 30);

-- Reconstruct the original data by joining the normalized tables
SELECT e.emp_id, e.emp_name, epa.hours_worked, p.project_id, p.project_name
FROM Employees e
INNER JOIN EmployeeProjectAssignments epa ON e.emp_id = epa.emp_id
INNER JOIN Projects p ON epa.project_id = p.project_id
ORDER BY e.emp_id, p.project_id;
4.4.2.name: SQL Exercise on 2NF

4.5.2.answer:
-- Create Customers Table
CREATE TABLE Customers (
    customer_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    customer_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    customer_address VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
);

-- Create Products Table
CREATE TABLE Products (
    product_id VARCHAR(10) PRIMARY KEY,
    product_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    price NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL
);



-- Create OrderDetails Table
CREATE TABLE OrderDetails (
    order_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    customer_id INT NOT NULL,
    product_id VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES Customers(customer_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES Products(product_id)
);

-- Insert data into Customers table
INSERT INTO Customers (customer_id, customer_name, customer_address) VALUES
(101, 'Alice', '123 Main St'),
(102, 'Bob', '456 Oak St'),
(103, 'Charlie', '789 Pine St');

-- Insert data into Products table
INSERT INTO Products (product_id, product_name, price) VALUES
('P02', 'Phone', 500.00),
('P03', 'Tablet', 300.00),
('P01', 'Laptop', 1000.00);

-- Insert data into OrderDetails table
INSERT INTO OrderDetails (order_id, customer_id, product_id) VALUES
(1, 101, 'P01'),
(2, 102, 'P02'),
(3, 101, 'P02'),
(4, 103, 'P03');

-- Reconstruct the original data by joining the normalized tables
SELECT c.customer_id, c.customer_name, c.customer_address, p.product_id, p.product_name, p.price, od.order_id
FROM OrderDetails od
INNER JOIN Customers c ON od.customer_id = c.customer_id
INNER JOIN Products p ON od.product_id = p.product_id
ORDER BY od.order_id ASC;
4.5.2.name: SQL Exercise on 3NF

FROM sales
GROUP BY category

4.7.16.answer:
SELECT category, SUM(quantity_sold) AS total_quantity_sold
FROM sales
GROUP BY category
HAVING SUM(quantity_sold) >= 30
ORDER BY category ASC;

    c.customer_id,
    c.email,
    c.address,
    o.order_date,
    o.total_amount
FROM customers c
INNER JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
ORDER BY c.customer_id, o.order_date DESC;

4.9.16.name: SQL Exercise on RIGHT JOIN

4.9.17.answer:
SELECT 
    od.product_id, 
    p.name, 
    o.customer_id, 
    o.total_amount, 
    od.unit_price 
FROM products p 
RIGHT JOIN order_details od ON p.product_id = od.product_id 
RIGHT JOIN orders o ON od.order_id = o.order_id;
4.9.17.name: Perform a Right Join on Product Details and Orders

4.9.19.name: SQL Exercise

4.9.21.name: SQL Exercise

4.9.23.answer:
SELECT p1.product_name, p2.product_name AS related_product_name, c.category_name
FROM products p1
INNER JOIN products p2 ON p1.category_id = p2.category_id AND p1.product_id <> p2.product_id
INNER JOIN categories c ON p1.category_id = c.category_id;
4.9.23.name: SQL Exercise

4.10.1.answer:
SELECT d1.dish_name as dish_name, d2.dish_name as related_dish_name, c.cuisine_name 
FROM dishes d1 
JOIN dishes d2 ON d1.cuisine_id = d2.cuisine_id 
JOIN cuisines c ON d1.cuisine_id = c.cuisine_id 
WHERE d1.dish_id != d2.dish_id;
4.10.1.name: SQL Exercise - Self Join - Restaurant

4.10.2.answer:
SELECT p.name, c.name AS category
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN product_categories pc ON p.product_id = pc.product_id
LEFT JOIN categories c ON pc.category_id = c.category_id;
4.10.2.name: SQL Exercise

4.10.3.name: SQL Exercise - Inner Join - Navy

4.10.4.answer:
SELECT
  s.station_id,
  s.name,
  s.region,
  wr.report_date,
  rd.value
FROM stations s
RIGHT JOIN weather_reports wr ON s.station_id = wr.station_id
RIGHT JOIN report_details rd ON wr.report_id = rd.report_id;
4.10.4.name: SQL Exercise	- Right Join 1 - Weather Monitoring Systemstat

4.10.5.name: Natural Join - Police Department

4.10.6.answer: SELECT police_officers.officer_id, police_officers.officer_name, police_officers.badge_number, officer_details.precinct, officer_details.years_of_service FROM police_officers CROSS JOIN officer_details;
4.10.6.name: Cross Join - Police Department

4.10.7.answer:
SELECT police_officers.officer_id, police_officers.officer_name, police_officers.badge_number, officer_details.precinct, officer_details.years_of_service
FROM police_officers
LEFT OUTER JOIN officer_details
ON police_officers.officer_id = officer_details.officer_id;
4.10.7.name: Left Outer Join - Police Department

4.10.8.answer: SELECT p.officer_id, p.officer_name, p.badge_number, o.precinct, o.years_of_service FROM police_officers p RIGHT OUTER JOIN officer_details o ON p.officer_id = o.officer_id;
4.10.8.name: Right Outer Join - Police Department

4.10.9.name: Cross Join - Satellite Communication

4.10.10.name: Left Outer Join - Satellite Communication

4.10.11.answer:
SELECT 
    s.satellite_id,
    s.satellite_name,
    s.launch_date,
    u.region,
    u.usage_hours
FROM satellite_info s
FULL OUTER JOIN satellite_usage u
    ON s.satellite_id = u.satellite_id;
4.10.11.name: Full Outer Join - Satellite Communication

4.10.12.answer:
SELECT 
    d1.device_name,
    d2.device_name AS related_device_name,
    dc.device_category_name
FROM devices d1
JOIN devices d2 
    ON d1.device_category_id = d2.device_category_id 
    AND d1.device_id != d2.device_id
JOIN device_categories dc 
    ON d1.device_category_id = dc.device_category_id
ORDER BY 
    dc.device_category_name, 
    d1.device_name, 
    d2.device_name;
4.10.12.name: Self Join - Satellite Communication

4.10.13.answer:
SELECT t1.toy_name, t2.toy_name AS related_toy_name, c.toy_category_name
FROM toys t1
JOIN toys t2 ON t1.toy_category_id = t2.toy_category_id
JOIN toy_categories c ON t1.toy_category_id = c.toy_category_id
WHERE t1.toy_id < t2.toy_id;
4.10.13.name: Self Join - Toy System

4.10.14.name: Navy Officer Database

4.10.15.answer: SELECT officer_id, officer_name, badge_number, base_location, years_of_service FROM air_force_officers NATURAL JOIN officer_details;
4.10.15.name: Air Force Personnel Database

4.10.16.answer:
SELECT 
*
FROM 
    class_A
LEFT OUTER JOIN 
    class_B ON class_A.id = class_B.id;
4.10.16.name: Left Outer Join

4.10.17.answer: SELECT customer_id, name, food_item, quantity, city FROM orders NATURAL JOIN customers;
4.10.17.name: Natural Join - Restaurant

4.10.18.name: Right Outer Join - Restaurant

4.10.19.answer:
SELECT customers.customer_name, orders.order_total, payments.amount_paid
FROM customers
FULL OUTER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id
FULL OUTER JOIN payments ON orders.order_id = payments.order_id;
4.10.19.name: FULL OUTER JOIN on Three Tables in PostgreSQL

4.10.20.answer:
SELECT innovation_name, category, material_name, amount
FROM innovations
LEFT JOIN materials ON innovations.innovation_id = materials.innovation_id
LEFT JOIN research_grants ON innovations.innovation_id = research_grants.innovation_id;
4.10.20.name: Retrieve Innovation Details with Sustainability Focus

4.10.21.answer:
SELECT a.act_name
FROM ACTOR a
JOIN MOVIE_CAST mc ON a.act_id = mc.act_id
JOIN MOVIES m ON mc.mov_id = m.mov_id
GROUP BY a.act_id, a.act_name
HAVING MIN(m.mov_year) < 2000 AND MAX(m.mov_year) > 2023;
4.10.21.name: Movie Database Management

4.10.22.answer:
SELECT destination_name, package_type, season_name
FROM destinations
CROSS JOIN travel_packages
CROSS JOIN seasons;
4.10.22.name: Travel Agency Packages

4.10.23.answer:
SELECT tour_packages.package_name, tour_packages.destination, customers.customer_name, bookings.booking_date
FROM bookings
RIGHT JOIN tour_packages ON bookings.package_id = tour_packages.package_id
RIGHT JOIN customers ON bookings.customer_id = customers.customer_id;
4.10.23.name: Finding Details of All Tour Packages

5.4.8.answer:
--Type Content here...
SELECT m.astronaut_name
FROM missions m
WHERE m.mission_score >= ANY (
    SELECT mission_score
    FROM missions
    WHERE astronaut_name IN (
        SELECT astronaut_name
        FROM astronauts
        WHERE station = 'Mars Station'
    )
);
5.4.8.name: Sub Queries - 4

5.4.9.answer:
--Type Content here...
SELECT cr.customer_name
FROM customer_rentals cr
WHERE cr.rating >= (
    SELECT MAX(cr2.rating)
    FROM customer_rentals cr2
    JOIN movie_details md ON cr2.movie_name = md.movie_name
    WHERE md.genre = 'action'
);
5.4.9.name: Sub Queries - 5

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION list_low_stock_products(threshold INT)
RETURNS SETOF VARCHAR(255) AS $$
    SELECT p.product_name
    FROM products p
    WHERE p.quantity < threshold
    ORDER BY p.product_id;
$$ LANGUAGE sql;

6.6.4.name: Procedures in PL/pgSQL

6.7.1.name: Procedure to Update Marks

6.7.2.answer:
-- Step 1: create the table
CREATE TABLE employees (
    emp_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    emp_name VARCHAR(100),
    salary DECIMAL(10, 2)
);

-- Step 2: Insert Sample Data into the `employees` Table
INSERT INTO employees (emp_id, emp_name, salary) VALUES
(1, 'John Doe', 50000),
(2, 'Jane Smith', 60000),
(3, 'Michael Johnson', 55000),
(4, 'Emily Brown', 52000),
(5, 'David Lee', 58000);


CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE UpdateSalaries(amount_increase DECIMAL)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
BEGIN
    UPDATE employees
    SET salary = salary + amount_increase;
END;
$$;
6.7.2.name: Procedure to Update Salaries 1

6.7.3.answer:
-- Step 1: Create the `menu_items` Table
-- This table will hold the menu items along with their prices.
CREATE TABLE menu_items (
    item_id INT PRIMARY KEY,       -- Unique identifier for each menu item
    item_name VARCHAR(100),        -- Name of the menu item
    price DECIMAL(10, 2)           -- Price of the menu item, stored as a decimal with 2 decimal places
);

-- Step 2: Insert Sample Data into the `menu_items` Table
-- Inserting 5 sample menu items with their respective prices
INSERT INTO menu_items (item_id, item_name, price) VALUES
(1, 'Spaghetti Bolognese', 150.00),
(2, 'Margherita Pizza', 120.00),
(3, 'Caesar Salad', 80.00),
(4, 'Cheeseburger', 100.00),
(5, 'Vegetable Stir Fry', 90.00);


CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE UpdateItemPrices()
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
BEGIN
    UPDATE menu_items
    SET price = price * 1.20;
END;
$$;
6.7.3.name: Increase the price by 20% of their current price

6.7.4.name: Procedure - Creating Simple Procedure 1

6.7.5.answer:
-- Step 1: Create the `students` Table
CREATE TABLE students (
    student_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    student_name VARCHAR(100),
    marks INT
);

-- Step 2: Insert Sample Data into the `students` Table
INSERT INTO students (student_id, student_name, marks) VALUES
(1, 'John Doe', 75),
(2, 'Jane Smith', 88),
(3, 'Michael Johnson', 90),
(4, 'Emily Brown', 82),
(5, 'David Lee', 95);

CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE UpdateMarksPercentage()
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
BEGIN
    UPDATE students
    SET marks = marks + (marks * 0.10);
END;
$$;
6.7.5.name: Increase the marks by 10% of their current marks
